name: Pong 1K 2P (NES homebrew)
_verified: 2026-06-08 — empirically verified by Wren playing the RIGHT paddle against Zeke; all fields cross-checked against the rendered display and a 5Hz RAM log across a full 9-0 match.

seats._note: Seat 2 == js0 (the dev virtual controller, gose_tap path, no explicit seat arg). Seat 1 == the human's physical pad. This assignment was verified live: driving js0 with no seat= moves p2_paddle_y (RIGHT paddle) while the human (Zeke) holds p1_paddle_y static.

up.effect: move paddle UP — DECREASES paddle_y on the AI's side (verified empirically)

start.effect: start a new game / advance past the title screen

controls._note: L/R inputs have no in-game effect (Pong is 1-axis). 'up' decreasing y is counter-intuitive but verified: held 'up' → p2_paddle_y moved 120→71. Use input.button(button='up'|'down', action='press'|'release') with no seat arg (routes to js0 = AI paddle).

p2_paddle_y.note: RIGHT paddle top-edge Y (AI seat 2 / js0). Paddles are 0x20 tall.

score_left.note: Score for the left side. Empirically the AI's score ends up here when AI=js0=right-paddle; verify against display if seat changes.

score_right.note: Score for the right side. Same caveat as score_left.

ball_speed.note: Ball speed / serve param (1-4). NOT a score — early drafts misread this.

frame_counter.note: Always-changing RNG/frame counter; useful as a liveness check.

game_flow.serve_detect: ball_x != 0 and ball_y != 0 — ball leaves the origin when a serve starts
game_flow.new_game: after game-over: send NCI RESET (UDP to RetroArch :55355) then tap 'start'; RESET sometimes needs 2-4 sends

ai_play_notes: Drive the RIGHT paddle (js0/default controller) by calling input.button with NO seat arg — exactly what gose_tap does.
Track ball_y reactively at ~15 Hz, or predict: project ball trajectory to the paddle's x-plane using velocity + wall-fold. See D:\gose-vm\wren_pong_p2.py for the verified implementation.
steer: compute target_y from prediction, then press/hold 'up' or 'down' to close the gap; release when within DEAD-BAND (±5 px).
score_left/score_right labels may be FLIPPED relative to physical left/right player depending on retroarch config; always cross-check against the display for a new session.
NCI RESET needs 2-4 UDP sends to reliably trigger a new game after game-over.
The seat/controller mapping is NOT trustworthy from config labels alone — always verify empirically (nudge the pad, watch which paddle moves) as shown in wren_pong_p2.py's auto-calibration.
